features.web-automation.hero_sub: Automate complex web interactions, scrape structured data, and bridge legacy web assets with modern conversational interfaces.
features.web-automation.general_bots_pro: General Bots provides the infrastructure to navigate, interact, and extract data from any web application using simple, deterministic BASIC logic.
features.web-automation.execute_multi_st: Execute multi-step web workflows invisibly using high-performance headless browser instances.
features.web-automation.transform_messy_: Transform messy HTML structures into clean, structured JSON data for immediate bot consumption.
features.web-automation.programmatically: Programmatically fill and submit web forms, including complex multi-page applications and login gates.
features.web-automation.not_every_busine: Not every business has an API. Many critical systems are still web-only — legacy ERPs, government portals, supplier dashboards — with no programmatic access. Web Automation bridges this gap by letting you control browsers programmatically, turning any website into an API.
features.web-automation.execute_multi_st: Execute multi-step web workflows invisibly using high-performance headless browser instances. Login, navigate, extract, and submit — all automated.
features.web-automation.transform_messy_: Transform messy HTML structures into clean, structured data for immediate consumption by bots and APIs. CSS selectors, XPath, and AI-assisted extraction.
features.web-automation.programmatically: Programmatically fill and submit web forms, including complex multi-page applications and login gates. Handle CAPTCHA, 2FA, and session management.
features.web-automation.think_of_it_as_a: Think of it as a robot that can use a web browser. You define the steps: navigate to this URL, log in with these credentials, click this button, extract that data, fill in this form, submit. The automation runs headlessly (no visible browser window) and can handle complex scenarios like multi-page workflows, file downloads, session management, and CAPTCHA handling. Each automation is defined as a BASIC script, making it easy to write, test, and modify. This is how companies automate supplier onboarding, extract competitor pricing, submit regulatory filings, and sync data between systems that don't talk to each other.
features.web-automation.automate_complex: Automate complex web interactions, scrape structured data, and bridge legacy web assets with modern conversational interfaces.
features.web-automation.headless_chromiu: Headless Chromium-based. Supports JavaScript, AJAX, and Single Page Applications. Run hundreds of automations concurrently.
features.content-generation.hero_sub: Generate on-brand, SEO-optimized content at scale. From technical documentation to marketing campaigns, ensure a unified voice across every channel.
features.content-generation.general_bots: General Bots leverages structured templates and intelligent data injection to produce content that isn't just generic AI text, but specialized business assets.
features.content-generation.define_struc: Define structure and constraints once; generate infinite variations that respect your guidelines.
features.content-generation.strict_enfor: Strict enforcement of tone, vocabulary, and compliance rules across all generated output.
features.content-generation.built_in_sem: Built-in semantic analysis ensuring content matches user intent and technical search requirements.
features.content-generation.content_crea: Content creation is the bottleneck for most marketing teams. You know what needs to be said, but writing it all — in the right tone, for the right audience, at the right length — takes time that most teams don't have. General Bots Content Generation speeds this up without sacrificing quality or brand consistency.
features.content-generation.define_struc: Define structure and constraints once in a template, then generate infinite variations that respect your brand guidelines. Templates enforce tone, vocabulary, and compliance rules across all output.
features.content-generation.strict_enfor: Strict enforcement of tone, vocabulary, and compliance rules. Every piece of generated content is checked against your brand standards before delivery.
features.content-generation.built_in_sem: Built-in semantic analysis ensures content matches user intent and search engine requirements. Keyword optimization, readability scoring, and meta description generation.
features.content-generation.the_secret_i: The secret is structured templates combined with AI. You define the guardrails — tone of voice, vocabulary, structural requirements, compliance rules — and the AI generates within those boundaries. A human reviews and approves before anything goes live. For a blog post, you specify the topic, target keywords, and length. For a product description, you provide the features and the AI writes the benefits. The system learns from your edits and gets better over time.
features.content-generation.ai_powered_c: AI-powered content creation with brand voice guardrails, atomic templates, and SEO optimization. Generate on-brand content at scale.
features.content-generation.generate_50_: Generate 50+ content variants per hour. Brand voice enforced across every piece. Human review loop built in.
features.create-apis-in-word.hero_sub: Write API logic using BASIC syntax directly inside Word documents. No IDE, no complex toolchains — just your logic, deployed instantly.
features.create-apis-in-word.general_bot: General Bots re-imagines the BASIC language for the modern era, allowing anyone to build deterministic backend endpoints using familiar tools.
features.create-apis-in-word.unlike_llm_: Unlike LLM generation, BASIC provides strict, predictable logic for mission-critical business rules.
features.create-apis-in-word.our_proprie: Our proprietary compiler transforms .docx structures into high-performance executable endpoints.
features.create-apis-in-word.modernize_s: Modernize SQL Server or Oracle databases by exposing them as REST JSON APIs via simple BASIC scripts.
features.create-apis-in-word.most_busine: Most business logic lives in documents. Policies, procedures, rules — they're written in Word files, PDFs, and emails. But they're not executable. General Bots changes this by letting you write API logic using BASIC syntax directly inside documents, turning your business rules into working endpoints.
features.create-apis-in-word.unlike_llm_: Unlike LLM generation, BASIC provides strict, predictable logic for mission-critical business rules. Every API endpoint behaves exactly as written, every time.
features.create-apis-in-word.our_compile: Our compiler transforms .docx structures into high-performance executable endpoints. Business analysts can define API logic using tools they already know.
features.create-apis-in-word.modernize_s: Modernize SQL Server, Oracle, or legacy databases by exposing them as REST JSON APIs via simple BASIC scripts. No middleware, no complex ETL pipelines.
features.create-apis-in-word.here_s_how_: Here's how it works: you write a BASIC script in a Word document, defining the logic step by step. SAVE a customer record. GET data from a database. CALL an external API. The compiler transforms your document into a compiled AST file that runs as a REST endpoint. No IDE, no deployment pipeline, no waiting for the engineering team. If you can write a numbered list of instructions, you can create an API. This is especially powerful for legacy system integration — expose mainframe or SQL Server data as modern JSON APIs without touching the original system.
features.create-apis-in-word.why_use_cre: Why Use Create APIs in BASIC
features.create-apis-in-word.build_rest_: Build REST APIs using BASIC syntax directly inside Word documents. No IDE, no complex toolchains — just logic, deployed instantly.
features.create-apis-in-word.compile_to_: Compile to executable AST in under 1 second. Full REST API with JSON responses. No server-side dependencies.
features.llm-tools.hero_sub: Integrate any LLM provider — DeepSeek, Qwen, GLM, Kimi, MiniMax, Yi — into your workflows. From summarization and translation to sentiment analysis and semantic caching.
features.llm-tools.general_bots_provides: General Bots provides the abstract layer needed to leverage multiple LLM providers simultaneously, ensuring zero vendor lock-in and optimized performance.
features.llm-tools.connect_to_openai_cla: Connect to DeepSeek, Qwen, GLM, Kimi, MiniMax, Yi, Step, Doubao — or any OpenAI-compatible API.
features.llm-tools.reduce_latency_and_co: Reduce latency and costs by up to 90% with intelligent caching that understands intent.
features.llm-tools.monitor_emotional_sta: Monitor emotional state in real-time and trigger human escalations based on frustration markers.
features.llm-tools.large_language_models: Large language models are incredibly powerful, but they have three problems: they're expensive, they hallucinate, and they can't access your internal data. LLM Tools solves all three by giving your models access to real-world tools — web search, calculators, databases, and your own custom APIs.
features.llm-tools.connect_to_openai_ant: Connect to DeepSeek, Qwen 2.5, GLM-4, Kimi, MiniMax, Yi, Step, Doubao, ERNIE — or any locally-hosted model via llama.cpp and Ollama. Switch providers with a single configuration change.
features.llm-tools.route_simple_queries_: Route simple queries to cheaper, faster models and complex reasoning tasks to high-tier models automatically. Optimize cost without sacrificing quality.
features.llm-tools.reduce_latency_and_co: Reduce latency and costs by up to 90% with intelligent caching that understands intent. Semantically similar queries return cached responses without calling the LLM.
features.llm-tools.the_approach_is_calle: The approach is called tool-augmented generation. Instead of asking the LLM to know everything from its training data, you give it tools it can call when needed. Need current stock prices? Call the market data API. Need to check inventory? Query the database. Need to send a confirmation email? Use the mail tool. Each tool is defined in BASIC and registered with the LLM. The model decides when to use each tool based on the user's request. This dramatically reduces hallucinations because the model is pulling real data instead of guessing. It also cuts costs because you can use smaller, cheaper models for most queries and only route complex ones to expensive frontier models.
features.llm-tools.unified_api_for_every: Unified API for every major LLM provider. Dynamic model routing, intelligent failover, semantic caching, and token cost optimization.
features.llm-tools.support_for_openai_an: Support for DeepSeek, Qwen, GLM, Kimi, MiniMax, Yi, and any OpenAI-compatible API. Open source, self-hosted, complete data privacy.
features.talk-to-data.hero_sub: Ask questions in natural language and get instant answers from your databases and spreadsheets. No SQL, no complex BI tools — just insights.
features.talk-to-data.stop_waiting_for_r: Stop waiting for reports. General Bots enables everyone in your organization to query data using the language they already speak.
features.talk-to-data.automatically_maps: Automatically maps technical database schemas to business concepts for intuitive querying.
features.talk-to-data.generates_complex_: Generates complex SQL on the fly, handling joins and filters with sub-second execution.
features.talk-to-data.the_bot_doesn_t_ju: The bot doesn't just return numbers; it synthesizes trends and provides context for the data.
features.talk-to-data.your_company_s_mos: Your company's most valuable asset is its data. But most people can't access it because they don't know SQL. Talk to Data removes this barrier entirely — anyone in your organization can ask questions in plain English and get answers from your databases, spreadsheets, and business systems.
features.talk-to-data.automatically_maps: Automatically maps technical database schemas to business concepts for intuitive querying. Users ask about 'sales by region' not 'SELECT SUM(amount) FROM orders GROUP BY region'.
features.talk-to-data.generates_complex_: Generates complex SQL on the fly, handling multi-table joins, filters, aggregations, and window functions with sub-second execution.
features.talk-to-data.the_bot_doesn_t_ju: The bot doesn't just return numbers — it synthesizes trends, highlights anomalies, and provides context for the data. Charts and visualizations generated automatically.
features.talk-to-data.the_system_works_b: The system works by mapping your database schema to business concepts automatically. A table called 'cust_orders' becomes 'customer orders.' A column called 'ord_total' becomes 'order total.' Users don't need to understand foreign keys, join conditions, or aggregate functions. They just ask: 'How many orders did we get last month?' or 'Show me sales by region for Q3.' The AI generates the SQL, executes it against your database, and returns the results as plain language with optional charts. If the result looks wrong, the user can ask follow-up questions to refine. It's like having a data analyst on call 24/7.
features.talk-to-data.query_databases_an: Query databases and spreadsheets using natural language. AI generates SQL, charts, and insights on the fly — no technical skills required.
features.talk-to-data.works_with_postgre: Works with PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, SQLite, and CSV/Excel files. Sub-second query execution.
features.bot-factory.hero_sub: Mass-produce bots from central templates, manage multi-tenant deployments, and scale your digital workforce from a unified dashboard.
features.bot-factory.the_bot_factory_use: The Bot Factory uses a unique "Parent-Child" architecture. Define a single PROMPT.md template and spawn thousands of isolated instances instantly.
features.bot-factory.update_the_master_t: Update the master template and all child bots receive the update instantly. No manual redeployment required.
features.bot-factory.complete_data_isola: Complete data isolation. Tenant A's customer data is cryptographically separated from Tenant B on the same core.
features.bot-factory.orchestrate_hundred: Orchestrate hundreds of specialized bots (HR, IT, Sales) under a single management plane.
features.bot-factory.running_one_bot_is_: Running one bot is easy. Running fifty — each with its own personality, knowledge base, and channel configuration — is a different story. That's why we built the Bot Factory: a system for creating, deploying, and managing bots at scale, from a single control plane.
features.bot-factory.define_a_single_pro: Define a single PROMPT.md and configuration template, then spawn thousands of isolated bot instances. Update the master template and all child bots receive the update instantly. No manual redeployment required.
features.bot-factory.complete_data_isola: Complete data isolation between tenants. Each organization's customer data, configuration, and conversations are cryptographically separated on the same core infrastructure.
features.bot-factory.orchestrate_hundred: Orchestrate hundreds of specialized bots — HR, IT, Sales, Support — under a single management plane. Each bot has its own personality, knowledge base, and channel configuration.
features.bot-factory.the_magic_is_in_the: The magic is in the template system. Define your bot's personality once in a master PROMPT.md, configure its tools and knowledge bases, then spawn as many instances as you need. When you update the master template, every child bot receives the change instantly. No SSH, no redeploys, no manual sync. Each bot still has its own isolated data and conversation history — tenants never see each other's information.
features.bot-factory.industrial_scale_bo: Industrial-scale bot deployment from central templates. Parent-child architecture with true multi-tenancy and instant propagation.
features.bot-factory.deploy_1_000_bots_f: Deploy 1,000 bots from a single template. Full multi-tenant isolation. Updates propagate in under 5 seconds.
features.ai-search.hero_sub: Traditional search matches keywords. General Bots AI Search understands meaning, providing precise answers derived from your documents.
features.ai-search.retrieval_augmented_g: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) ensures that your AI responses are grounded in your actual business data, eliminating hallucinations and ensuring accuracy.
features.ai-search.high_performance_vect: High-performance vector indices return semantic matches in under 200ms across millions of documents.
features.ai-search.every_answer_includes: Every answer includes clickable citations to the source documentation, building trust with users.
features.ai-search.ask_questions_in_any_: Ask questions in any language and get answers derived from documents in any other language.
features.ai-search.every_business_has_do: Every business has documents — PDFs, Word files, Excel spreadsheets, internal wikis. The problem has never been storage; it's finding the right information when you need it. General Bots AI Search solves this by understanding the meaning behind your questions, not just matching keywords.
features.ai-search.powered_by_qdrant_vec: Powered by Qdrant vector database and high-fidelity embeddings, AI Search delivers semantic matches in under 200ms across document collections of any size. Unlike keyword search, it understands meaning — finding answers even when users phrase questions differently than the source text.
features.ai-search.every_answer_includes: Every answer includes clickable citations pointing to the exact source document and passage. Build trust with users who can verify AI-generated responses against your original content.
features.ai-search.documents_in_any_lang: Documents in any language can be queried in any other language. The embedding model captures semantic relationships across languages, making your knowledge base accessible to a global audience.
features.ai-search.think_about_the_last_: Think about the last time you searched for a file on your company's shared drive. You probably knew the answer was in there somewhere, but the search tool couldn't find it because you used different words than the document. AI Search uses vector embeddings to map your documents into a semantic space where meaning drives results, not exact word matches. A search for 'quarterly revenue trends' will find documents about 'financial performance by quarter' because the system understands the concepts are related.
features.ai-search.high_performance_sema: High-performance semantic search across millions of documents using Retrieval-Augmented Generation. Vector embeddings, cited sources, multilingual support.
features.ai-search.200ms_average_respons: 200ms average response time across 10M+ documents. Supports PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, TXT, HTML, and scanned images via OCR.
features.broadcast.hero_sub: Run targeted campaigns, schedule messages across time zones, and track engagement with real-time analytics across all your channels.
features.broadcast.broadcast_is_more_tha: Broadcast is more than just sending messages. It's about starting meaningful conversations with your audience, grounded in precision targeting.
features.broadcast.automatically_pace_de: Automatically pace delivery to comply with provider limits, protecting your account health.
features.broadcast.target_users_based_on: Target users based on conversation history, technical attributes, or custom enterprise tags.
features.broadcast.transition_engaged_us: Transition engaged users directly into bot flows or human handoff workflows instantly.
features.broadcast.sending_a_message_to_: Sending a message to one person is easy. Sending a personalized message to ten thousand people — each with the right tone, timing, and channel — without getting flagged as spam? That requires a proper broadcast engine. General Bots Broadcast handles the complexity so you can focus on the message.
features.broadcast.automatically_pace_me: Automatically pace message delivery to comply with provider limits and protect your account health. WhatsApp, SMS, and email channels each have provider-specific rate profiles that Broadcast manages automatically.
features.broadcast.target_users_based_on: Target users based on conversation history, behavior patterns, demographic attributes, or custom tags. Create precise segments that update in real time as user data changes.
features.broadcast.when_a_recipient_repl: When a recipient replies to a broadcast, the system automatically transitions them into a bot conversation flow or human handoff workflow. Turn one-way announcements into two-way engagements.
features.broadcast.the_biggest_mistake_c: The biggest mistake companies make with mass messaging is treating everyone the same. Broadcast lets you segment your audience by behavior, conversation history, demographics, or custom tags — then send each segment a message that feels personal. The system automatically paces delivery to stay within provider rate limits (WhatsApp, SMS, email each have their own), and when someone replies, they're routed into a conversation flow automatically. It turns one-way announcements into two-way relationships.
features.broadcast.targeted_multi_channe: Targeted multi-channel campaigns with intelligent rate limiting, dynamic segmentation, and automated reply loops.
features.broadcast.send_100k_messages_pe: Send 100K+ messages per hour. Automatic rate limiting per channel. Real-time engagement tracking.
features.trainning.hero_sub: Build specialized bots by training them on your proprietary documents, FAQs, and structured datasets. Turn enterprise knowledge into active intelligence.
features.trainning.general_bots_provides: General Bots provides an industrial pipeline for document ingestion, semantic chunking, and vector indexing, ensuring your bot always has the most accurate information.
features.trainning.intelligent_document_: Intelligent document splitting that preserves context, ensuring the LLM receives the most relevant information snippets.
features.trainning.update_word_or_pdf_do: Update Word or PDF documents and see the bot's knowledge update instantly without manual re-indexing or downtime.
features.trainning.process_image_heavy_d: Process image-heavy documents and complex Excel tables with built-in parallel extraction engines.
features.trainning.a_bot_is_only_as_good: A bot is only as good as its knowledge. If your bot doesn't know your products, policies, and procedures, it will give wrong answers. Training is the process of teaching your bot by feeding it your documents — and General Bots makes this trivially simple.
features.trainning.intelligent_document_: Intelligent document splitting that preserves context and paragraph boundaries. Each chunk maintains coherence, ensuring the LLM receives the most relevant information.
features.trainning.update_word_or_pdf_do: Update Word or PDF documents and see the bot's knowledge update instantly. No manual re-indexing, no downtime, no rebuilds.
features.trainning.process_image_heavy_d: Process image-heavy documents and complex tables with built-in parallel extraction engines. PDFs, scanned documents, and Excel files all become searchable knowledge.
features.trainning.you_don_t_need_to_lab: You don't need to label data, create training sets, or fine-tune models. Just drop your documents into a .gbkb folder — PDFs, Word files, Excel spreadsheets, plain text — and the system handles the rest. It automatically chunks the documents into meaningful segments, generates embeddings using your chosen model, and indexes them in Qdrant for fast semantic search. When a user asks a question, the system finds the most relevant chunks and includes them in the prompt to the LLM. If you update a document, the knowledge base updates automatically — no manual re-indexing required. This is true continuous learning: your bot gets smarter as you add more documents.
features.trainning.train_bots_on_your_pr: Train bots on your proprietary documents, FAQs, and datasets. Automated chunking, vector indexing, and hot-swappable knowledge bases.
features.trainning.index_100k_documents_: Index 100K+ documents per hour. Automatic chunking and embedding. Hot-swappable knowledge bases.

resources.community.basic-course.basic_w: BASIC was designed in 1964 at Dartmouth College with a single purpose: to make programming accessible to people who were not computer scientists. That purpose is more relevant today than ever. The people who know what a bot should say, how it should handle errors, and what data it should retrieve are domain experts — support agents, HR managers, operations analysts — not software engineers. General Bots uses BASIC because it lets the people who understand the work also program the tool that does the work. A BASIC program reads like pseudocode: each line does one thing, and the entire program fits in a single screen.
resources.community.basic-course.the_bas: The BASIC Course progresses from conversational flows to full LLM tool integration. Module one covers the fundamentals: dialog trees, user input handling, and response formatting. Module two introduces data operations: reading from databases, calling REST APIs, and transforming results. Module three covers LLM integration: defining tools that the language model can call, managing conversation context, and handling multi-step reasoning. Module four addresses production concerns: error handling, logging, testing, and deployment. Each module includes hands-on exercises that build on the previous one, culminating in a complete, production-ready bot.
resources.community.basic-course.the_cou: The course is not an academic exercise — it is a production on-ramp. By the end of Module four, participants have a working bot deployed on a real channel with real users. The course emphasizes the same patterns used in production deployments: modular BASIC scripts, tool abstractions that separate business logic from LLM interaction, and configuration-driven channel setup. Participants who complete the course can independently build, modify, and operate General Bots deployments. They do not need to wait for engineering resources, file tickets, or rely on external consultants. They own the full stack from conversation design to deployment.

resources.community.become-a-partner.the: The General Bots partner ecosystem connects technology companies, system integrators, and hosting providers who build, deploy, and support AI bot solutions on the General Bots platform. Partners benefit from a growing market of organizations seeking open-source AI automation, a platform that eliminates vendor lock-in for their clients, and a technology stack that reduces delivery time from months to weeks. The ecosystem operates on a principle of shared value: partners deliver client success, and the platform grows through reputation, not restrictive licensing.
resources.community.become-a-partner.cer: Certified hosting partners operate General Bots instances for clients who prefer managed deployment over self-hosting. Certification ensures that partners meet infrastructure requirements, maintain platform updates, and provide SLA-backed support. Hosting partners set their own pricing, define their own service tiers, and own the client relationship. General Bots provides the platform, documentation, and technical support channel. The hosting partner provides the operations, monitoring, and first-line support. This model has proven effective for organizations in regulated industries that require AI capabilities but lack internal DevOps capacity.
resources.community.become-a-partner.sol: Solution partners build vertical AI bot applications on General Bots — industry-specific packages that combine BASIC scripts, LLM configurations, and channel integrations into turnkey solutions. Integration partners connect General Bots to enterprise systems: ERPs, CRMs, ticketing platforms, and document management systems. Both partner types benefit from the platform open architecture: there are no proprietary APIs to license, no platform fees per deployment, and no restrictions on how solutions are packaged or priced. The only requirement is that partner solutions remain compatible with the General Bots runtime so clients retain portability.

resources.already-using.data-science.gen: General Bots turns data access from a specialist task into a conversational interaction. The Talk to Data feature lets business users ask questions in natural language and receive answers drawn directly from databases, spreadsheets, and documents — no SQL, no Python, no dashboard configuration required. Under the hood, the system translates natural language queries into structured data operations, executes them, and presents results in readable format. Data scientists configure the data sources and define the semantic layer; business users interact through conversation.
resources.already-using.data-science.ret: Retrieval-Augmented Generation is the backbone of accurate, grounded AI responses. General Bots includes built-in vector storage and retrieval that indexes your documents, web pages, and knowledge bases. When a user asks a question, the system retrieves the most relevant passages and feeds them to the LLM as context, producing answers that cite source material rather than inventing information. The vector retrieval pipeline supports chunking strategies, embedding model selection, and similarity thresholds — all configurable without code changes.
resources.already-using.data-science.for: For data operations that go beyond simple retrieval, BASIC scripts provide a programmable layer between the user and the data. A BASIC tool can execute database queries, call external APIs, transform results, apply business rules, and format the output — all within a single, readable script. This approach replaces the typical data pipeline stack of notebooks, schedulers, and microservices with one file that any team member can read, modify, and deploy. Data scientists write the logic once; the bot executes it on demand, at scale, across any channel.

resources.already-using.essential-resour: Running bots in production requires operational visibility. General Bots includes built-in tools for monitoring bot health, inspecting conversation logs, and tracking LLM token consumption. The admin dashboard shows real-time metrics: active conversations, average response times, error rates, and channel-specific usage. Conversation logs are searchable and filterable, making it possible to diagnose issues, audit responses, and identify improvement opportunities. Token consumption tracking helps teams manage LLM costs before they become surprises.

Beyond official documentation, the General Bots community contributes templates, tutorials, and solution patterns. The template marketplace provides plug-and-play bot packages for common use cases — customer support, knowledge retrieval, appointment scheduling, and data analysis. Community tutorials walk through real-world deployments with step-by-step instructions. The Help Center aggregates frequently asked questions and known issues with resolutions. When you encounter a problem, chances are someone in the community has already solved it and shared the solution.

The open-source BotServer runs on any Node.js environment — local machine, cloud VM, or on-premises server. Clone the repository, install dependencies, and run. The default configuration includes a working bot with a Web Chat channel, a BASIC script editor, and an admin dashboard. From there, you connect your preferred LLM provider, add channels, and write your first BASIC program. No sales calls, no credit card, no trial expiration. The full platform is available from day one.
resources.considering.onboard-support.fo: For teams that want hands-on assistance, our certified hosting partners offer guided onboarding programs. These are not generic consulting engagements — they are structured sprints designed to get a production bot live within two to four weeks. The onboarding covers environment setup, channel configuration, LLM provider selection, BASIC script development for your first use case, and a handoff that leaves your team fully autonomous. resources.considering.customers.general_: General Bots serves organizations that need AI automation without surrendering control of their data, their code, or their infrastructure. Our customers span financial services, insurance, retail, healthcare, and government — industries where data sovereignty, regulatory compliance, and vendor independence are not optional features but baseline requirements. They choose General Bots because it is the only open-source bot platform that delivers production-grade AI with BASIC programmability, self-hosted deployment, and no per-message licensing fees.
resources.considering.customers.customer: Customer retention in the bot platform market is low because most platforms create dependency: proprietary scripting languages, locked-in hosting, and API changes that break production workflows. General Bots customers stay because the platform reduces dependency instead of increasing it. BASIC programs are readable, transferable, and modifiable by anyone on the team. Self-hosted deployment means no vendor can hold your data hostage. Open-source code means you can fix, extend, or replace any component. The organizations that stay with General Bots are the ones that value autonomy over convenience.
resources.considering.customers.general_: General Bots deployments range from single-department pilots to organization-wide rollouts. The common thread is not company size but problem clarity: our most successful customers start with a well-defined use case — answering repetitive customer questions, retrieving internal knowledge, processing routine transactions — and expand from there. The platform scales horizontally by adding bots and channels, not by rewriting code. A bot that works on WhatsApp can be deployed on Microsoft Teams, Web Chat, or SMS with configuration changes alone.

resources.considering.case-studies.a_mid: A mid-size insurance company deployed General Bots on WhatsApp to handle policy inquiries, claim status checks, and appointment scheduling. Before the bot, the contact center handled 1,200 calls per day with an average wait time of 8 minutes. After deployment, 62% of inquiries were resolved by the bot without human escalation, average wait time dropped to 2.5 minutes, and customer satisfaction scores increased by 18 points. The bot was live within three weeks using a single BASIC script that connected to the existing policy management API.
resources.considering.case-studies.a_fin: A financial services firm with 3,000 employees used General Bots to build an internal knowledge assistant on Microsoft Teams. Employees ask questions about compliance procedures, HR policies, and IT support. The bot retrieves answers from vector-indexed documents and, when needed, executes BASIC tools to look up live data from internal systems. The firm estimates 4,200 hours saved per quarter — time previously spent searching intranets, reading PDFs, and waiting for email replies. The system was built by a three-person team with no prior AI experience.
resources.considering.case-studies.a_ret: A retail chain operating across physical stores, e-commerce, and a WhatsApp catalog integrated General Bots to unify order tracking across all channels. Customers can check order status, request returns, and get product recommendations through any channel. The bot handles 15,000 interactions per month with a 91% resolution rate. The same BASIC business logic runs identically on WhatsApp, Web Chat, and the e-commerce widget, eliminating channel-specific code duplication and ensuring consistent customer experience.

resources.considering.ai-powered-org.bec: Becoming an AI-powered organization is not about adopting a single technology — it is about building the capability to identify, implement, and iterate on AI use cases across every business function. The transformation starts with a clear-eyed assessment of where AI can deliver measurable value and where it cannot. High-volume, pattern-rich processes are the best candidates: customer support, document processing, data extraction, and routine decision-making. Low-volume, high-judgment processes are the worst: strategic planning, creative direction, and relationship management.
resources.considering.ai-powered-org.a_s: A successful AI implementation follows a phased approach that balances quick wins with long-term capability building. Phase one deploys a single, well-scoped bot — typically customer support or internal knowledge retrieval — on a single channel. Phase two expands to multiple channels and adds tool integration so the bot can take actions, not just provide information. Phase three extends AI across business functions with orchestrated multi-bot deployments that share infrastructure, data, and governance.
resources.considering.ai-powered-org.tec: Technology adoption fails without organizational readiness. The most common failure mode is not technical — it is cultural. Teams that see AI as a threat resist adoption; teams that see it as a tool accelerate it. Change management for AI requires transparent communication about what the technology will and will not do, hands-on training that lets employees experience the tool firsthand, and feedback mechanisms that give teams agency over how AI integrates into their workflows. General Bots' BASIC programming model is itself a change management tool — it allows the people closest to the work to modify the AI's behavior, turning potential resistors into active contributors.

general-bots.llm.why_pay_for_big_tech_wh: Why Pay for Big Tech When You Can Have Choice?
general-bots.llm.the_ai_market_is_domina: The AI market is changing fast. Open models from China — DeepSeek, Qwen, GLM, Kimi, MiniMax — now rival the best proprietary systems at a fraction of the cost. General Bots puts you at the forefront of this shift: self-hosted, private, open source, and completely free from vendor lock-in.
general-bots.llm.open_source_bilingual_m: Open-source bilingual model (Chinese/English) with strong reasoning capabilities. GLM-4-9B rivals GPT-3.5 on many benchmarks. Free to self-host, no per-token costs. Ideal for organizations that need Chinese language support or want a capable open-weight model without recurring fees.
general-bots.llm.one_of_the_strongest_op: One of the strongest open-weight model families available. Qwen 2.5 72B competes with GPT-4 on multiple benchmarks. Available in sizes from 0.5B to 110B. The 32B and 72B variants run efficiently on consumer-grade GPUs. No API costs, no usage limits.
general-bots.llm.the_breakout_model_of_2: The breakout model of 2025. DeepSeek V3 matches GPT-4 on reasoning and coding at roughly 5% of the API cost. DeepSeek R1 introduces chain-of-thought reasoning that rivals o1. Fully open weights — run on your own hardware, no per-token fees, complete data privacy.
general-bots.llm.kimi_excels_at_long_con: Kimi excels at long-context processing with a 2M+ token window — read entire codebases, book-length documents, or months of conversation history in a single pass. Strong Chinese and English support. Available via API at competitive pricing.
general-bots.llm.minimax_offers_competit: MiniMax offers competitive performance on par with GPT-3.5 at significantly lower cost. Their Hailuo model family excels at text generation, code completion, and structured output. Strong option for cost-sensitive deployments that don't need frontier-model capabilities.
general-bots.llm.we_don_t_exclude_the_bi: The Chinese AI ecosystem is vast. Yi (01.AI) delivers competitive coding performance, Step (StepFun) excels at multimodal reasoning, Doubao (ByteDance) leads in consumer AI, and ERNIE (Baidu) offers deep enterprise integration. Add any OpenAI-compatible API — one interface, every model, your rules.
general-bots.llm.why_pay_20_user_month_f: Open-source models deliver world-class performance at near-zero marginal cost. No per-seat fees, no per-token pricing, no data training on your prompts.
general-bots.llm.general_bots_llm_orches: General Bots' LLM orchestrator is built in Rust, not Python. This means zero-copy token handling, lock-free concurrent request processing, and memory-safe execution. The orchestrator handles thousands of simultaneous streaming requests across multiple model providers with sub-millisecond routing overhead.
